database_info.name: SIB Standalone Access Control Selection Theory

1_standalone_vs_networked.principle: Standalone controllers are self-contained; networked controllers connect to central management

comparison.best_for: Single doors, small offices, gates, storage rooms
Enterprise, multi-door, audit trail, integration with video

1_standalone_vs_networked.recommendation: SIB for budget single-door installations. Hikvision for enterprise multi-door with central management and audit trail.

2_controller_selection.principle: Controller type based on authentication method and environment

guide.best_for: Remote management via smartphone, Airbnb, rental
Smart locks, co-working spaces, modern offices

3_magnetic_lock_sizing.principle: Holding force must exceed maximum expected force on door

guide.use: Maximum security doors, bank vaults, heavy gates

3_magnetic_lock_sizing.wiring: Standard: 2-wire (12VDC power). 5-wire: adds door position detection signal (recommended for monitored systems).

4_bracket_selection.principle: Bracket type depends on door opening direction and frame type

guide.function: Extends lock body to align with door edge
Z-bracket on frame + L-bracket on lock for inward swing
Clamps around glass edge to mount armature plate

4_bracket_selection.sizing: Match bracket to lock size: L180B-L for 180KG lock, L280B-ZL for 280KG lock, etc.

5_electric_lock_types.principle: Lock type depends on door type and fail mode requirement

types.best_for: Fire exits, emergency doors — code required
types.note: Must release on power failure for fire safety

types.best_for: Standard doors with mechanical lock — electric release

5_electric_lock_types.fire_safety: For fire exit doors: ALWAYS use fail-safe locks (unlock on power loss). Mag locks are inherently fail-safe. Electric bolts must be configured as fail-safe.

6_exit_button_selection.principle: Exit button type based on environment, aesthetics, and hygiene

guide.best_for: Hospitals, cleanrooms, hygiene-critical areas, COVID-aware installations
Emergency exit — fire regulations require break-glass adjacent to mag-locked fire doors

7_em_vs_mifare_cards.principle: Card technology affects security and compatibility

comparison.compatible: All SIB EM models, most standalone readers
SIB MF models, Hikvision Mifare readers

7_em_vs_mifare_cards.recommendation: Mifare for any installation where card cloning is a concern. EM for budget/low-security applications.

8_uhf_long_range_access.principle: UHF readers provide hands-free long-range vehicle access

system.cards: C01-UHF card ($1.50) or LB01-UHF windshield sticker ($1.50)

8_uhf_long_range_access.use_case: Parking garage entry, compound gates, logistics yards — vehicle drives through and gate opens automatically without stopping.
8_uhf_long_range_access.integration: UHF reader relay output → gate barrier controller (Hikvision DS-TMG series) or SIB standalone controller relay input.

9_complete_door_system_design.principle: Each door requires a complete set of components

standard_door_kit.lock: 1× Magnetic lock (L280B for standard door) + appropriate bracket (L/ZL/U)
standard_door_kit.power: 1× 12VDC 3A power supply (or access control PSU with battery backup)

9_complete_door_system_design.cost_example: Budget door: K1201EM ($8) + OP07 ($1.25) + L180B ($9) + L180B-L bracket ($3) + sensor ($1) + PSU ($4.50) = ~$27 per door

standards.scope: Fail-safe locks mandatory on fire exit doors
